steps.title: Find the semicircle size
steps.content:
Since $O$ is the center of the semicircle and $NP$ is the diameter, $PO$ is the radius. Thus the radius is $52$ feet, and
$$NP=2(52)=104$$

steps.title: Find the rectangle size
steps.content:
Point $P$ is the midpoint of $NQ$, so $NP=PQ$. Therefore
$$NQ=104+104=208$$
The rectangle has dimensions $208$ feet by $18$ feet.

steps.title: Compute the expression
steps.content:
Add the semicircle area and the rectangle area.
$$\frac{1}{2}\pi(52)^2=1{,}352\pi$$
$$208(18)=3{,}744$$
So $a=1{,}352$ and $b=3{,}744$. Then
$$a-b=1{,}352-3{,}744=-2{,}392$$
The answer is -2392.
